req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three distinct decision paths: (1) a standard deposit below $5,000 that auto-approves to 'pending', (2) a deposit at or above $5,000 that queues manual verification, and (3) an image that fails MICR decode and returns 422. BVA targets the $5,000 threshold with amounts of $4,999.99, $5,000.00, and $5,000.01. EP partitions the input space into valid-low, valid-high, and invalid-image classes. NEG covers the MICR decode failure. DT captures the compound rule: amount × image-quality → routing decision. STC traces the deposit lifecycle from 'uploaded' through 'pending' or 'queued-for-review'.

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_returns_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A well-formed cheque image with readable MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $1,000.00 (representative of the valid-low equivalence class below $5,000)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=1000.00
Assert response body contains a non-null deposit_id
expected: No manual verification queue entry created
trace: R-FIN-019: cheque deposit via mobile image
R-FIN-019: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification (inverse: below threshold does not)
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_returns_pending(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 1000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is False
    assert body.get("deposit_id"), "deposit_id must be present"

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_threshold_queues_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A well-formed cheque image with readable MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $10,000.00 (representative of valid-high equivalence class above $5,000)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=10000.00
Assert decisioning queue entry is created via GET /deposits/{deposit_id}
trace: R-FIN-019: cheque deposit via mobile image
R-FIN-019: cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_threshold_queues_manual_review(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 10000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True
    deposit_id = body.get("deposit_id")
    assert deposit_id, "deposit_id must be present"
    review_resp = auth_session.get(f"/deposits/{deposit_id}")
    assert review_resp.status_code == 200
    assert review_resp.json().get("review_status") == "queued"

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_invalid_image_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A corrupted or unreadable image that will fail MICR decode is prepared
actions: Assert HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned
Assert error code in response body identifies MICR decode failure
trace: R-FIN-019: 422 when image fails MICR decode (error pathway)
R-FIN-019: image uploaded trigger with invalid image
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_invalid_image_returns_422(auth_session, invalid_micr_image):
    payload = {
        "image": invalid_micr_image,
        "amount": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error_code" in body
    assert body["error_code"] == "MICR_DECODE_FAILURE"
    assert body.get("deposit_id") is None

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_exact_threshold_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A well-formed cheque image with readable MICR line is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=5000.00
expected: Boundary rule: amount >= $5,000 triggers manual verification
trace: R-FIN-019: cheques ABOVE $5,000 require manual verification — boundary at exactly $5,000.00
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_exact_threshold_requires_manual_review(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 5000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True, "Exactly $5000 must trigger manual review"
    assert body.get("deposit_id"), "deposit_id must be present"

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_one_cent_below_threshold_no_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A well-formed cheque image with readable MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $4,999.99 (one unit below boundary)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=4999.99
expected: Boundary rule: $4,999.99 does NOT trigger manual verification
trace: R-FIN-019: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification — off-boundary at $4,999.99
R-FIN-019: below threshold should not queue decisioning
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_one_cent_below_threshold_no_manual_review(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 4999.99,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is False, "$4999.99 must NOT trigger manual review"
    assert body.get("deposit_id"), "deposit_id must be present"

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_state_transition_pending_to_queued
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A well-formed cheque image with readable MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $7,500.00 to trigger manual review path
Deposit has been successfully submitted and deposit_id obtained
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=7500.00
Assert state transitions from 'uploaded' through 'pending' to 'queued'
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 202 with status='pending'
State machine: uploaded -> pending -> queued (no skipped states)
trace: R-FIN-019: expected outcomes = deposit pending; decisioning queued
R-FIN-019: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification — lifecycle state coverage
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_state_transition_pending_to_queued(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 7500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    post_resp = auth_session.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 202
    body = post_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True
    deposit_id = body.get("deposit_id")
    assert deposit_id, "deposit_id must be present"
    get_resp = auth_session.get(f"/deposits/{deposit_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    detail = get_resp.json()
    assert detail.get("review_status") == "queued"
    assert detail.get("status") == "pending"
